summary: Deterministic proof that claim discovery keeps portable binary labels and possible-evidence preflight separate from Cautilus Agent or review-result interpretation.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: claim discovery label, preflight, and review-result boundary
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the contract claims about portable binary audience labels, cheap deterministic preflight, and review-result-gated evidence upgrades are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: The binary performs semantic duplicate merging or final product interpretation.
Possible evidence refs satisfy claims by themselves.
Unsupported audience labels or evidence-status upgrades are accepted.
Cautilus Agent has already reviewed every possible evidence ref.
Every future semantic grouping taxonomy is binary-owned.

protectedBehaviors: claim discovery emits portable audience labels and semantic group hints
adapter-owned audience hints can classify user/developer sources
missing semantic groups fall back to General product behavior
review input can include possibleEvidenceRefs without changing evidenceStatus from unknown
verified review results can update satisfied evidence
satisfied evidence is rejected without verified evidence refs
review results, not raw binary discovery, can update claimAudience
adapter claim discovery config accepts portable audience hints
unknown claim-discovery audience hints are rejected

decision.reason: Focused runtime and adapter tests prove the binary emits only portable audience labels, keeps richer semantic grouping as hints/review work, attaches possible evidence as preflight rather than satisfaction, and requires verified review-result/evidence-ref semantics before evidenceStatus can become satisfied.
